text: Ventilatory assistance includes noninvasive methods (eg, high-flow nasal cannula (HFNC), CPAP, BPAP) and invasive methods (intubation with mechanical ventilation). [ A in Context Link 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 ]

A specialty society clinical guideline, based upon a systematic review of the evidence, concludes that heart failure patients with evidence of significant fluid overload should be promptly treated with IV loop diuretics (eg, initial dose twice the home regimen in terms of oral to parenteral milligram equivalents) and titrated to resolution of clinical signs and symptoms of congestion (eg, serial doubling of dosage as bolus or continuous infusion). ( 1 ) This guideline further recommends increasing the diuretic dose or adding a second diuretic (eg, thiazide diuretic or aldosterone antagonist with diuretic properties) until an effective regimen is identified that is adequate to relieve congestion. ( 1 ) A pediatric guideline states that diuretics are first-line therapy for children with heart failure and fluid overload. ( 4 ) [ B in Context Link 1 , 2 , 3 ]

The most accurate estimate of the GFR requires a steady state (stable) serum creatinine. ( 12 ) ( 13 ) However, the clinical setting of acute decompensated heart failure usually does not allow for delayed estimation (ie, waiting for a stable creatinine level). Further, in most instances, estimation in the setting of a rising creatinine will underestimate the true decline in GFR. ( 12 ) Accepted definitions of acute kidney injury speak to an acute change (rise) in serum creatinine and use these changes as part of the process of defining the stage (severity) of kidney injury. ( 13 ) For the purposes of level of care determination, the point is that the degree of heart failure is such that acute kidney injury has occurred or is impending, and therefore inpatient care and monitoring are appropriate. [ C in Context Link 1 , 2 ]

Routine use of pulmonary artery catheters is not recommended for adults or children admitted with acute heart failure. ( 1 ) ( 4 ) Pulmonary artery catheterization (right-sided heart catheterization) may be needed when the diagnosis is unclear (eg, suspected heart failure with left ventricular ejection fraction greater than 40%), for patients with ongoing symptoms or uncertain fluid status following initial diuretic treatment, or to guide device selection and utilization of mechanical circulatory support. ( 1 ) ( 14 ) ( 15 ) [ D in Context Link 1 , 2 ]

A specialty society guideline recommends 4 medication classes as guideline-directed medical therapy (GDMT) for adult patients with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF): beta-blockers, renin-angiotensin inhibitors (angiotensin receptor-neprilysin inhibitor preferred), aldosterone blockers, and sodium-glucose cotransporter-2 inhibitors. ( 1 ) This same guideline recommends the following for patients hospitalized with HFrEF absent clear contraindications: pre-existing GDMT should be continued and optimized (titration to therapeutic dose begun as inpatient), GDMT should not be discontinued due to mild decrease in renal function or blood pressure, GDMT should be initiated during hospitalization when clinical stability has been achieved (if all drug classes not on board), and, if GDMT is discontinued, it should be reinstated as soon as possible. ( 1 ) Vericiguat further reduces the risk of adverse cardiac outcomes in heart failure and may either be started with the standard quadruple therapy outlined above for severe worsening heart failure, used as a substitute agent for patients with contraindication or intolerance to one or more standard medications, or used as an add-on therapy for inadequate response to optimal quadruple therapy. ( 18 ) For heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF), an expert panel recommends sodium-glucose cotransporter-2 inhibitors, aldosterone blockers, and either an angiotensin receptor-neprilysin inhibitor (preferred) or an angiotensin receptor blocker as GDMT, with the addition of beta-blockers reserved for specific indications, including comorbid atrial fibrillation, myocardial infarction within 3 years, or angina. ( 22 ) [ E in Context Link 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 ]

For children with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, a specialty society guideline recommends routine use of renin-angiotensin inhibitors, with the addition of diuretics for patients with fluid retention and ventricular dysfunction; other drugs that are recommended as reasonable additional therapies include beta-blockers, digoxin, and ivabradine. ( 4 ) For children who have heart failure with preserved ejection fraction, the same guideline recommends diuretics to achieve euvolemia and recommends considering a sodium-glucose cotransporter-2 inhibitor for symptoms. ( 4 ) [ F in Context Link 1 ]

Lifestyle interventions include high-protein diets for malnutrition; weight-loss diets, exercise, and anti-obesity medications for obesity; exercise and movement (including cardiac rehabilitation); and evaluation and treatment for sleep-disordered breathing, depression, and anxiety. ( 4 ) ( 23 ) ( 24 ) [ G in Context Link 1 ]

ICU care is indicated for acute respiratory failure, cardiogenic shock, severe arrhythmias, evidence of cardiac ischemia, worsening acute kidney injury, or need for pulmonary artery catheter, IV inotrope or vasopressor, or mechanical circulatory support. ( 1 ) ( 2 ) ( 4 ) ( 7 ) ( 9 ) ( 14 ) ( 31 ) ( 32 ) ( 33 ) See Intensive Care Guidelines ISC for further information. [ H in Context Link 1 ]
See Intermediate Care Guidelines ISC for further information. [ I in Context Link 1 ]
See Clinical Indications for Admission to Inpatient Care in this guideline. [ J in Context Link 1 ]
Electrolyte monitoring may be indicated if patient is actively diuresing. ( 1 ) [ K in Context Link 1 ]
Some patients may have their hydration needs met via alternative means (eg, percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy tube). [ L in Context Link 1 ]
Discharge instructions should be given in the patient's and caregiver's native language using trained language interpreters whenever possible. ( 45 ) [ M in Context Link 1 ]
